about.title: Who We Are
about.subtitle: Strengthening management for a healthier Bangladesh
about.lead: Partners in Health and Development (PHD) is a national non-profit development organization in Bangladesh with over 30 years of experience.
about.body: We work in public health, humanitarian response, climate resilience, gender equality, research, capacity development, and partnership management. PHD works closely with government institutions, UN agencies, development partners, NGOs, and communities to improve the health and wellbeing of vulnerable and marginalized populations across Bangladesh.
about.mission: To improve the health and wellbeing of vulnerable and marginalized populations by strengthening management and building local capacity.
about.vision: A Bangladesh where every community has equitable access to quality health and development services.
about.values: Integrity, accountability, equity, partnership, and a relentless focus on impact.

where.title: Where We Work
where.subtitle: Reaching vulnerable and marginalized communities across Bangladesh.
where.body: From the coastal climate-vulnerable belt to remote rural districts and urban informal settlements, our programs span the country in partnership with local and national stakeholders.
where.reachSubtitle: Working across all eight divisions of Bangladesh.

approach.title: How we create lasting impact
approach.subtitle: A proven, partnership-driven approach — from understanding needs to sustaining results.
approach.s1Desc: We start with communities, partners and evidence to understand real needs.
approach.s2Desc: We co-design solutions with local stakeholders and government.
approach.s3Desc: We deliver programs while building local capacity and systems.
approach.s4Desc: We measure impact and hand over ownership for lasting change.
approach.focusSubtitle: Our work spans the issues that matter most to vulnerable communities.
